characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The Ace: He always comes out on top. Battle Cry: "To the power of the pack!" and "Let's hit the road, Rovers!" Catchphrase:"Yetanotherunexpected twist, bummer!""I wouldnothave predicted this!""[X], cool." "Yetanotherunexpected twist, bummer!" "I wouldnothave predicted this!"

characters.name: Hunter
characters.description: The Ace: He always comes out on top. Battle Cry: "To the power of the pack!" and "Let's hit the road, Rovers!" Catchphrase:"Yetanotherunexpected twist, bummer!""I wouldnothave predicted this!""[X], cool." "Yetanotherunexpected twist, bummer!" "I wouldnothave predicted this!"

characters.name: Colleen
characters.description: Action Girl: She does a lot of fighting. Alpha Bitch: A literal one, to Blitz, anyway. The guy may be a jerk, but roundhouse-kicking your coworker in the teeth whenever he does or says something that annoys you can't be good for the group's dynamics. Catchphrase: (generally in a sarcastic tone) "There's your comedy." Deadpan Snarker: She's a bit more cynical than the others and tends to make remarks about her teammates' shortcomings and quirks. Double Standard: Abuse, Female on Male: Whenever she kicksBlitz' ass, it's always treated as comical and a well-deserved comeuppance for... Whatever he just did to incite her ire.

characters.name: Exile
characters.description: Ambiguously Gay: There are a few clues to this.In "Hair of the Dog that Bit You," we get to see Exile's room and the decor is a little feminine. Even including a vanity and an ornate bed."Two shakes of a lamb's jiffy!" and when Hunter asked what that was, Colleen replied "Don't ask..."Exile's Christmas Song "Jingle Balls" which includes the line "Jingle all the Balls!" He had never heard of Jingle Bells either.When Sport joined the Rovers, Exile's immediate reaction to welcome him was to kiss him. Then he offered to give all his comrades a big kiss. Rather than turning to give Colleen the first of those kisses, he turned to Blitz instead.His fainting spell after assisting Colleen in surgery.He has said the phrase "I love you" to his fellow Rovers, but only the male ones. Never to Colleen.However, in one episode when Blitz made a love confession to Exile that was intended for Colleen, Exile told him to seek therapy. This could just mean he isn't into Blitz. In "Hair of the Dog that Bit You," we get to see Exile's room and the decor is a little feminine. Even including a vanity and an ornate bed. "Two shakes of a lamb's jiffy!" and when Hunter asked what that was, Colleen replied "Don't ask..." Exile's Christmas Song "Jingle Balls" which includes the line "Jingle all the Balls!" He had never heard of Jingle Bells either. When Sport joined the Rovers, Exile's immediate reaction to welcome him was to kiss him. Then he offered to give all his comrades a big kiss. Rather than turning to give Colleen the first of those kisses, he turned to Blitz instead.

characters.name: Blitz
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: Towards Colleen, to which she usually responds with violence Accidental Misnaming: Colleen's favorite way of getting back at Blitz, aside from kicking his ass, is to completely screw up his name. In the final episode, she says his actual name, but returns to the usual routine when he starts being a jerk again. The Ahnold: He's physically fit and speaks in an Austrian accent. Butt Biter: Oh so much. He's kind of obsessed with biting people on their backsides, which weirds out Exile. Butt-Monkey: So, so much. Often courtesy of Colleen.

characters.name: Shag
characters.description: The Big Guy: He's physically the largest and also very strong. Eyes Out of Sight: His eyes are always covered by fur. Full-Frontal Assault: Likely do to the sheer size & volume of his fur, Shag typically goes without the standard Road Rover outfit and works unclothed. Hairy Hammerspace: He often has stuff hidden in his fur. In one episode Colleen manages to hide in there. Intelligible Unintelligible: Everyone can understand him except the viewer.

characters.name: Muzzle
characters.description: Ax-Crazy: A rare heroic example, as he'll violently attack anyone he's released upon."Reigning Cats And Dogs" reveals this is the result of Parvo's failed experiment on him during the Professor's early attempts to create the Transdogmafier. Big Eater: He's often shown with a full belly after he's done attacking the bad guys. Expy: He was obviously inspired by Hannibal Lecter fromThe Silence of the Lambs. Extreme Omnivore: Is implied to devour the bad guys he attacks. Gory Discretion Shot: All of Muzzle's attacks are offscreen. And if the Rovers' reactions are anything to go by,it's better they are.

characters.name: The Master
characters.description: Big Good: He created the Road Rovers and is the one who sends them on missions. Blank White Eyes: He mentions in one episode that they're the results of special effects. Handwave: At one point, The Master claims that his glowing white eyes are possible because of special effects. Thissidestepsa better look at him, as he may or may not have been maimed in the first episode. Mr. Exposition: He informs the Road Rovers of the wrongs they must right. Super-Empowering: He's the one who invented the Transdogmafier that turns the Rovers into their humanoid forms.

characters.name: The Groomer
characters.description: The Dragon: She is Parvo's second-in-command and does most of the physical work. Groundhog Peggy Sue: Due to the show'sStable Time Loopin "Reigning Cats and Dogs", she ends upgoing back in time to Parvo's transformation, giving him his helmet to cover his cat ears, and even give him his new name.Temporal Paradox: The implication being that shetravels back in time, gets to the end of the show, and goes back, repeating forever...without ever having existed outside the loop. Temporal Paradox: The implication being that shetravels back in time, gets to the end of the show, and goes back, repeating forever...without ever having existed outside the loop. Right-Hand Hottie: She's quite attractive.

characters.name: Zachary Storm
characters.description: General Ripper: It's implied that he got kicked out of the military for firing against orders. Motive Misidentification: Subverted. When Zachary taunts the Rovers by having them guess why he's destroying ten key U.S. cities, Hunter blurts out that Zachary is mad at the NFL for letting the Clevland Browns move to Baltimore. The subversion comes in when Zacharyactually thinks about itand realizes he is mad about it, but says that he has another reason for his actions.

characters.name: Donovan Bell
characters.description: Corrupt Corporate Executive: Runs a dog kidnapping ring.

characters.name: Sport
characters.description: The Mole: An unwilling one, anyway. Parvo forced him to infiltrate the Road Rovers to lure them to him.
